name: hotspot
description: Predicts bug-prone files by fusing git churn, bug-fix history, code complexity, code ownership, and change coupling into an inline 0–100 risk score. 100% local & offline.

colors.description: Explorer badge color for critical-risk files.
Explorer badge color for high-risk files.
Explorer badge color for medium-risk files.

hotspot.scanOnStartup.description: Run a hotspot scan automatically when a git workspace is opened. Reuses the cached churn when HEAD is unchanged.

hotspot.topHotspotsCount.description: How many top-ranked files to list in the “Show Top Hotspots” quick pick.

hotspot.codeRiskEnabled.description: Highlight risky code regions (deeply-nested / long blocks) in the editor with a gutter marker and a hover explanation.

hotspot.codeRiskMinSeverity.description: Only highlight code-risk regions at or above this severity.

hotspot.codeLensEnabled.description: Show a per-region risk CodeLens (⚠ Risk: severity · depth · lines) above risky code blocks, gated by the same minimum severity as the gutter decorations.

hotspot.explainEnabled.description: Explain *why* a file scored as it did: decompose its risk into each signal's % share and a plain-language dominant driver, shown in the Risk Report tooltip, the Show Top Hotspots quick pick, and the exported report. Turn off to hide the breakdown.

hotspot.exclude.description: Glob patterns (** , * , ? , /) for paths excluded from the risk ranking — generated, vendored, and lockfile paths that carry churn but no actionable risk. Set to [] to disable exclusion.

churn.description: Weight of code churn (lines added + deleted).

recency.description: Weight of time-decay recency (recently-changed files rank higher).

ownership.description: Weight of ownership fragmentation (1 − top-author share).

coupling.description: Weight of change coupling (strongest temporal co-change).

hotspot.weights.description: Relative weights of the additive risk signals. Defaults sum to 1.0; any missing or invalid key falls back to its default.

medium.description: Score at/above which a file is at least medium risk.

high.description: Score at/above which a file is at least high risk.

critical.description: Score at/above which a file is critical risk.

hotspot.thresholds.description: Score cutoffs (0–100) for the low/medium/high/critical tiers. Invalid keys fall back to defaults.

hotspot.sinceMonths.description: Limit the git-history window to the last N months (0 = all history). Smaller windows emphasize recent activity and speed up the scan.

scripts.pretest: npm run compile-tests && npm run build
